task_completion.description: Create at least three interior partition walls and two doors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan view of Sweet Home 3D, look for at least three newly drawn interior wall segments that divide the shell, and at least two door icons placed within these walls to allow access between the separated areas.

task_completion.description: Define and label at least four distinct zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan view, look for at least four distinct room areas (indicated by room area shading/text) with visible text labels such as 'Woodshop', 'Electronics Lab', '3D Print Farm', 'Textile Studio', 'Common Workspace', or 'Tool Storage'.

task_completion.description: Apply different floor colors or textures to at least three zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D view pane (usually the bottom half of the screen) or the 2D plan, verify that at least three of the defined rooms have distinct, different floor colors or patterns applied to them.

task_completion.description: Furnish the space with at least 10 workbenches/tables, 10 chairs/stools, and 8 storage units.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan or 3D view, observe a large quantity of furniture. Count or estimate at least 10 tables/workbenches, 10 seating items (chairs/stools), and 8 storage items (cabinets, shelves, lockers).

task_completion.description: Add at least 6 lighting fixtures, 3 decorative items, ensuring at least 40 total items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan or 3D view, identify at least 6 lighting fixtures (ceiling lights, lamps) and 3 decorative items (plants, posters, etc.). The overall space should look densely furnished, consistent with having 40 or more total items.

task_completion.description: Save the completed layout to overwrite the starter file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Sweet Home 3D window title bar to ensure it reads 'makerspace_starter.sh3d' (without an unsaved changes asterisk at the end of the trajectory), or look for a save dialog confirming the overwrite of 'makerspace_starter.sh3d'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application interface (catalog, 2D plan, 3D view) being actively used, rather than terminal commands or text editors modifying the .sh3d file directly.
The sequence of screenshots should show walls being drawn, rooms being defined, and furniture being dragged and dropped step-by-step, rather than the sudden appearance of a fully completed layout.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the correct starter file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen opening and working within 'makerspace_starter.sh3d' rather than creating a completely new project from scratch and renaming it at the very end.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target output file is 'makerspace_starter.sh3d'. The task requires 100 total points with a pass threshold of 70. Required features include furniture placement (>=40 items total: 10+ tables, 10+ chairs, 8+ storage, 6+ lighting, 3+ decor), wall creation (>=3 interior walls), room definition (>=4 labeled zones), floor color (>=3 different floor colors/textures), and door placement (>=2 doors).
